reason: identifiers.py: baseline snapshot (module_size=121, max_complexity=4, avg_complexity=1.63) predates the committed state of the file at 80d8a18 (#299) -- the file was never edited after that commit (git log --follow shows a single introducing commit) so there is no debt to pay down, only a stale snapshot. The 12 non-blank lines and CC 4->5 delta come from ProgramName.__new__'s three independent path-traversal guards (empty, dot-component, separator) -- see docstring at identifiers.py:68-70. Corrected module_size, max_complexity, avg_complexity to match the already-merged file; all other metrics (method_ratio, encapsulation_ratio, avg_params, classes_per_module, class_to_func_ratio, violations, future_annotations) were already at parity and are untouched.

Lock in the claude_md.py decomposition (prior commit) that the baseline did not yet record. Tighten src/punt_vox/claude_md.py from its pre-split snapshot (module_size 379, classes_per_module 2, avg_complexity 2.55, avg_params 1.0) to the current post-split metrics (module_size 110, classes_per_module 1, avg_complexity 2.17, avg_params 0.83), and add first-time entries for the three extracted modules atomic_file.py (140), managed_section.py (121), markdown_fence.py (60) -- each clearing every OO threshold. Values are per-file oo_score.py output. Scoped to the 4 touched files; the 37 un-baselined voxd/programs/ modules are a separate pre-existing hygiene issue and are intentionally left alone (no blanket make update-oo).

Lock in the AtomicFile consolidation (prior commit) that the ratchet baseline did not record. keys_env.py delegated its hand-rolled atomic writer to AtomicFile: tighten from the pre-refactor snapshot (max_complexity 20, avg_complexity 10.5, module_size 140, avg_params 1.2) to current (6, 3.11, 108, 1.11). Lock atomic_file.py module_size 140->123 and filesystem_store.py module_size 156->151 (both improved by the consolidation). atomic_file.py avg_params (0.83) and avg_complexity (2.0) are left as set for the forced-mode parameter -- legitimately grown feature substance, already at current. Values are per-file oo_score.py output. Scoped to the 3 touched files; the 37 un-baselined voxd/programs/ modules are a separate hygiene issue, left alone (no blanket make update-oo).

atomic_file.py: the fdopen-failure cleanup path now wraps its temp unlink in contextlib.suppress(OSError) so a raising unlink can't mask the original os.fdopen error (parity with the main cleanup path). That fix and its explanatory comment add 3 non-blank lines (module_size 123->126); every other metric is unchanged. Scoped correction, not a blanket update-oo.

Reconcile the 29 previously-untracked voxd/programs/ modules into the OO baseline -- a baseline-completeness gap #307/#308 deliberately left alone (their scoped JSON-only lock-ins avoided a blanket update-oo that would have swept these in). Every one of the 29 clears every absolute OO threshold (module_size <= 300, max_complexity <= 10, method_ratio >= 0.8, class_to_func_ratio >= 0.5, encapsulation 1.0, zero init/public-attr violations, future annotations present) -- no debt is blessed; each is baselined at its shipped, already-clean values. Largest is service.py (module_size 268, max_complexity 6). Also drop the stale migrate.py entry: the file was deleted per the no-migration policy and no longer exists on disk. Values are per-file oo_score.py --json output. JSON-only commit scoped to voxd/programs -- no blanket make update-oo, other files' baselines untouched.
public Python API exports (VoxClient, VoxClientSync, SynthesisSpec, SynthesizeResult) grow the package __init__ past its version-only stub
extracted _validate_and_infer to remove a duplicated resolve+infer branch (max_complexity 11->9, avg_complexity 5->4); the shared 3-arg helper nudges avg_params 2.5->2.6
